QF.id: Income, Residential Status, Five Heads of Income_8

question.text: Under section 17 of the Income Tax Act, which of the following is NOT included in the definition of "salary"?

answer.text: Pension received from a former employer
Dividend income from shares allotted under an ESOP

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 17 defines "salary" to include wages, pension, gratuity, fees, commission, perquisites, advance of salary, and profits in lieu of salary (including ESOPs at the time of exercise). However, dividend income earned on shares — even if those shares were acquired through an ESOP — is taxable under "Income from Other Sources," not under "Salary." The ESOP benefit (difference between FMV and exercise price) is a perquisite under salary, but subsequent dividends are a separate income stream. **The Trap:** Candidates often confuse the ESOP perquisite (taxable as salary at exercise) with subsequent income from those shares (dividend = other sources, capital gains = capital gains head). **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series VIII (Equity Derivatives) and Series X-B both test ESOP taxation from different angles. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When advising clients with ESOPs, break the tax into three stages: (1) exercise = salary perquisite, (2) holding = dividend under other sources, (3) sale = capital gains. Each falls under a different head of income.
